title: Doctorate in Business Administration (DBA)

hero.tagline: Lead Research. Shape Industries. Earn the Pinnacle Credential.

overview.description: The Doctorate in Business Administration (DBA) at IMBS is designed for senior professionals seeking to bridge high-level practice with rigorous academic research. This programme develops executive scholars who can create original knowledge, lead transformational change, and influence global business strategy. Delivered entirely online, the DBA empowers you to pursue world-class doctoral credentials without stepping away from your career.
targets: Senior executives and C-suite leaders seeking a research credential
Business consultants and advisors aiming to publish applied research
Academics and trainers wishing to formalise their doctoral standing
Entrepreneurs looking to validate and scale their business models through research
Professionals targeting teaching or advisory roles at reputed institutions

outcomes: Conduct original applied research that contributes to global business knowledge
Lead doctoral-level strategic analysis across complex organisational environments
Publish research in peer-reviewed journals and industry publications
Influence policy and strategy at executive and board levels
Qualify for faculty and advisory roles at international business schools

faqs.question: Is the DBA recognised internationally?
faqs.answer: Yes. The DBA from IMBS is awarded under internationally accredited standards and recognised by professional bodies across multiple countries. We recommend verifying recognition in your specific jurisdiction.

faqs.question: Do I need to attend any classes physically?
faqs.answer: No. The programme is delivered entirely online, including supervisor meetings, workshops, and viva voce sessions conducted via secure video conferencing.

faqs.question: Can I work while pursuing the DBA?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The DBA is specifically designed for senior working professionals. The self-paced learning model allows you to study at your convenience without disrupting your career.

faqs.question: What is the minimum eligibility for DBA admission?
faqs.answer: You must hold any Master's degree from a recognised institution and have a minimum of 2 years of post-graduate professional work experience.

faqs.question: How is the dissertation assessed?
faqs.answer: Your dissertation undergoes a two-stage evaluation: written submission review by an internal and external examiner panel, followed by a Viva Voce (oral defense) conducted online.

faqs.question: Can I pay the fees in instalments?
faqs.answer: Yes. The total programme fee is split across four semester-wise instalments to ease your financial planning. Refer to the fee structure section for details.

testimonials.name: Rajeev Sharma
testimonials.text: The DBA programme gave me the framework to convert 20 years of industry experience into rigorous academic research. My dissertation has since been cited in two industry journals.

testimonials.name: Priya Menon
testimonials.text: Pursuing the DBA online while managing a team of 200 professionals was seamless. The flexible schedule and expert supervisors made all the difference.

title: Doctorate in Management Studies (DMS)

hero.tagline: Advance Management Science. Drive Organisational Excellence.

overview.description: The Doctorate in Management Studies (DMS) is a practitioner-focused research doctorate tailored for management professionals who wish to investigate complex organisational phenomena and develop actionable knowledge. Through rigorous scholarly inquiry and applied research, DMS graduates emerge as thought leaders equipped to drive meaningful change across industries, policy environments, and academic institutions.
targets: Mid-to-senior managers aiming to formalise their expertise through a doctoral credential
HR, operations, and general management professionals targeting leadership research
Management trainers and coaches seeking academic recognition
Public sector leaders and administrators advancing management practice
Professionals planning a transition into academic or policy advisory roles

outcomes: Design and execute original management research with scholarly rigor
Apply advanced quantitative and qualitative methods to real-world management issues
Publish in peer-reviewed management and organisational journals
Lead evidence-based transformation programmes in large organisations
Contribute to management education and curriculum design
career_pathways: Chief People Officer / Organisational Development Lead

faqs.question: What is the difference between DBA and DMS?
faqs.answer: The DBA focuses on advanced strategic business research, while the DMS centres on management science, organisational behaviour, and systems-level inquiry. Both are doctoral credentials but differ in research orientation.

faqs.question: Is any on-campus attendance required?
faqs.answer: No. The entire DMS programme, including mentorship sessions and the final oral defense, is delivered and conducted online.

faqs.question: What research support will I receive?
faqs.answer: Each student is assigned a dedicated academic supervisor for one-on-one mentorship throughout the dissertation journey, supplemented by group research workshops every trimester.

faqs.question: How many hours per week does the programme require?
faqs.answer: On average, students spend 10–15 hours per week on coursework, literature review, and research activities. The flexible format accommodates full-time working professionals.

faqs.question: Can international students apply?
faqs.answer: Yes. The DMS programme is open to eligible candidates from all countries. Tuition can be paid in INR or USD depending on your location.

faqs.question: What happens if I need to take a break mid-programme?
faqs.answer: IMBS offers a formal leave of absence policy allowing students to pause for up to one semester with prior written approval, without forfeiting progress or fees already paid.

testimonials.name: Dr. Sunita Agarwal
testimonials.text: The DMS gave me a structured lens to research workplace culture transformation. My findings are now embedded in our company's global HR strategy.

testimonials.name: Michael Osei
testimonials.text: As someone managing cross-border teams, the DMS research modules on organisational dynamics were directly applicable. A truly transformative experience.

title: Masters Program in Business Administration

hero.tagline: Master Business Strategy. Lead with Confidence. Grow Globally.

overview.description: The Masters Program in Business Administration (MBA) at IMBS is a comprehensive, industry-aligned programme designed to equip graduates with the managerial acumen, analytical depth, and leadership capabilities required to thrive in today's competitive global economy. Spanning functional and strategic domains, this programme blends theory with practical application through case studies, simulations, and a capstone industry project.
targets: Fresh graduates aspiring to fast-track their management career
Working professionals looking to transition into management roles
Entrepreneurs seeking structured business management knowledge
Government and non-profit professionals targeting leadership positions
Sales and marketing executives aiming for general management roles

outcomes: Apply core management principles across functional business domains
Develop and execute data-driven marketing, financial, and operational strategies
Lead teams effectively through change and uncertainty
Analyse competitive landscapes and formulate corporate strategy
Communicate business insights to executive and board-level stakeholders

faqs.question: Do I need a specific undergraduate degree to apply for the MBA?
faqs.answer: No. Any graduation from a recognised university qualifies you for admission to the MBA programme at IMBS, regardless of your undergraduate discipline.

faqs.question: Is work experience required for MBA admission?
faqs.answer: Work experience is not mandatory for the MBA. However, candidates with professional experience tend to derive deeper value from the case-study intensive curriculum.

faqs.question: How is the MBA programme assessed?
faqs.answer: Assessment is a combination of assignments, online examinations, case analysis submissions, and a final capstone industry project evaluated by faculty and industry mentors.

faqs.question: Will I receive a degree certificate upon completion?
faqs.answer: Yes. Upon successful completion, you will receive an internationally accredited Masters in Business Administration degree certificate issued by IMBS.

faqs.question: Is the MBA programme available in multiple specialisations?
faqs.answer: The core MBA covers all major management domains. Specialisation electives in Finance, Marketing, HR, and Operations are embedded in Semesters 3 and 4.

faqs.question: Can I access study materials offline?
faqs.answer: Yes. All course materials, lecture notes, and recorded sessions are available for download via the IMBS Learning Portal for offline access.

testimonials.name: Ananya Krishnan
testimonials.text: The IMBS MBA gave me the confidence to move from a purely technical role into a leadership position. The marketing and strategy modules were game-changers.

testimonials.name: Samuel Adebayo
testimonials.text: Studying online from Nigeria with world-class faculty was something I didn't expect to be possible. IMBS made it seamless and genuinely impactful.

title: Masters in Computer Application

hero.tagline: Engineer the Future. Master Computing at the Highest Level.

overview.description: The Masters in Computer Application (MCA) at IMBS is a rigorous postgraduate programme designed to produce highly skilled computer science professionals capable of designing, developing, and managing complex software systems and technology solutions. With a curriculum covering advanced programming, AI/ML, cloud computing, and full-stack development, MCA graduates are equipped for high-demand roles across the global tech industry.
targets: Science and commerce graduates looking to build a career in IT
Working IT professionals seeking to formalise and advance their technical expertise
Software developers targeting senior or architect-level positions
System administrators and network engineers moving into software development
Aspiring data scientists, cloud architects, and AI engineers

outcomes: Design and develop scalable, secure software applications and systems
Apply AI and machine learning techniques to solve real-world problems
Architect cloud-native solutions on leading cloud platforms
Lead software development teams using agile and DevOps methodologies
Analyse system performance and implement optimisation strategies

faqs.question: Do I need a computer science background to enrol in MCA?
faqs.answer: No. Any graduate is eligible. However, candidates with prior exposure to basic programming or mathematics tend to progress faster through the early modules.

faqs.question: What programming languages are taught in the MCA?
faqs.answer: The curriculum covers Python, Java, JavaScript (Node.js/React), and SQL as core languages, with exposure to tools like Docker, TensorFlow, and cloud SDKs.

faqs.question: Are there live coding sessions or only recorded lectures?
faqs.answer: The programme includes both pre-recorded lectures for self-paced study and scheduled live coding labs conducted via interactive online platforms.

faqs.question: Is the MCA programme useful for someone already working in IT?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The MCA is designed to sharpen advanced competencies in AI, cloud, and software architecture that are highly valued for mid-to-senior tech career progression.

faqs.question: What is the final project requirement?
faqs.answer: In Semester 4, each student completes an industry-grade software project, documented with architecture diagrams and code repositories, followed by an online oral defense.

testimonials.name: Vikram Nair
testimonials.text: The MCA modules on cloud computing and AI were exactly what I needed to move from a junior developer role to a solutions architect position within a year.

testimonials.name: Aisha Mubarak
testimonials.text: IMBS offered a world-class MCA curriculum at an accessible price point. The machine learning track was both rigorous and immediately applicable.

title: Executive Masters in Business Management (EMBA)

hero.tagline: Designed for Leaders. Built for Impact. Accelerated for Success.

overview.description: The Executive Masters in Business Management (EMBA) is an accelerated, leadership-focused programme engineered specifically for experienced professionals ready to step into senior management and executive roles. Condensing the most critical elements of general management into 18 intensive months, the EMBA sharpens your strategic thinking, financial fluency, and digital leadership capabilities — all while you continue building your career.
targets: Mid-career managers with 2+ years of experience targeting leadership roles
Professionals transitioning from technical to management or executive tracks
Business owners seeking formal management frameworks for scale
Government and NGO professionals targeting senior administrative roles
Sales directors, project managers, and operations leads seeking an executive credential

curriculum.title: Digital Transformation & Innovation
topics: AI, Automation & the Future of Work

outcomes: Lead strategic planning and execution at the organisational level
Navigate complex financial decisions with confidence and data fluency
Drive digital transformation and cultural change across business units
Build and lead diverse, high-performing executive teams
Develop and present comprehensive business strategies to senior stakeholders

faqs.question: Is the EMBA different from the regular MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The EMBA is accelerated (18 months vs 24 months), requires prior work experience, and focuses on leadership, strategy, and executive decision-making rather than foundational management concepts.

faqs.question: How much work experience do I need to apply?
faqs.answer: A minimum of 2 years of professional work experience post-graduation is required. Candidates with more experience often get more out of the peer learning and case study discussions.

faqs.question: Is the EMBA suitable for business owners?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Many EMBA participants are founders or co-founders who use the programme to implement structured management frameworks in their growing businesses.

faqs.question: What makes the EMBA accelerated?
faqs.answer: The EMBA is compressed into 18 months by focusing exclusively on senior management essentials, eliminating foundational modules present in the regular MBA, and using an intensive semester schedule.

faqs.question: Is there a group project component?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Capstone in Semester 3 may be completed individually or as a small cohort team, simulating a real executive committee solving a strategic business challenge.

testimonials.name: Rohan Desai
testimonials.text: The EMBA was exactly what I needed at my career stage. Compact, high-impact, and directly applicable to the boardroom conversations I'm part of every week.

testimonials.name: Fatima Al-Hassan

title: Masters Program in Public Health

hero.tagline: Protect Populations. Shape Health Policy. Lead the Change.

overview.description: The Masters Program in Public Health (MPH) at IMBS is an interdisciplinary graduate programme that prepares health professionals, administrators, and researchers to address complex public health challenges at local, national, and global levels. Grounded in epidemiology, health systems, policy analysis, and community health research, the MPH equips graduates to design and implement evidence-based interventions that improve health outcomes at scale.
targets: Healthcare professionals (doctors, nurses, paramedics) seeking health management expertise
Public health officers and government health administrators
NGO and international development professionals working in health sectors
Life sciences and pharmacy graduates targeting health policy roles
Researchers and academics interested in global health and epidemiology

curriculum.title: Research Project in Public Health
topics: Research Design for Public Health Studies

outcomes: Design and evaluate evidence-based public health programmes and interventions
Analyse epidemiological data to identify disease trends and risk factors
Formulate and advocate for effective health policies at multiple governance levels
Manage public health departments, NGO health divisions, or international health projects
Contribute to global health research and disease surveillance systems

faqs.question: Do I need to be a doctor or nurse to enrol in the MPH?
faqs.answer: No. The MPH is open to any graduate. While healthcare professionals frequently enrol, candidates from social sciences, management, and life sciences also benefit greatly from the programme.

faqs.question: Is the MPH useful for working in international health organisations?
faqs.answer: Yes. The global health module specifically covers WHO, UNICEF, CDC, and other international health governance structures, making MPH graduates well-suited for such organisations.

faqs.question: Does the MPH include fieldwork or practicum?
faqs.answer: The Research Project in Semester 4 incorporates a community health assessment component. For online students, this may be conducted in their local community with virtual supervisor oversight.

faqs.question: Can I specialise within the MPH?
faqs.answer: The MPH programme covers core public health competencies across all domains. Elective depth is offered in epidemiology, health management, or global health in Semesters 3 and 4.

faqs.question: Is this programme suitable for government health officials?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Health Policy & Systems Management module and the Capstone are particularly valuable for government health administrators managing public healthcare delivery.

testimonials.name: Emmanuel Mensah

overview.description: The Graduate Program in Management Studies (GMS) is a three-year undergraduate degree programme that equips students with core management competencies, business acumen, and professional skills needed to launch a successful career in any industry. Designed for 12th-pass students ready to enter the world of business, the GMS combines foundational management theory with practical project-based learning and industry exposure.

First-generation college students aspiring to business and management careers

faqs.question: Can I apply for GMS directly after completing Class 12?
faqs.answer: Yes. The GMS is specifically designed for students who have passed 12th grade from any recognised board. No prior college education is required.

faqs.question: Is this a degree programme?
faqs.answer: Yes. Upon successful completion of all three years, you will be awarded a Graduate Degree in Management Studies, an internationally accredited undergraduate credential.

faqs.question: What kind of jobs can I get after GMS?
faqs.answer: GMS graduates are well-prepared for management trainee, sales, HR, retail, and administrative roles across industries. Many graduates also go on to pursue an MBA.

faqs.question: How is the programme delivered?
faqs.answer: The GMS is delivered 100% online through the IMBS Learning Portal, with recorded lectures, live webinars, and downloadable study materials available at all times.

faqs.question: Can I pursue an MBA after completing GMS?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The GMS serves as the ideal foundation for progression to the MBA or Executive MBA programmes offered by IMBS or other institutions.

overview.description: The Graduate Program in Business Administration (GBA) is a structured three-year undergraduate business degree that provides students with a thorough grounding in all key pillars of business — from marketing, finance, and operations to strategy, law, and entrepreneurship. faqs.question: What is the difference between GMS and GBA?
faqs.answer: The GMS focuses more broadly on management theory and people skills, while the GBA has a stronger emphasis on business administration, law, finance, and operations — both lead to comparable undergraduate-level credentials.

faqs.question: Can I work while completing the GBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme is entirely online and self-paced, allowing you to study on weekends, evenings, or during personal time without affecting your work schedule.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance tests or interviews for admission?
faqs.answer: No entrance test is required. Admission is based on 12th pass certification and completion of the online application form.

faqs.question: Is the GBA equivalent to a BBA?
faqs.answer: The GBA is a bachelor-level programme in business administration comparable to a BBA in curriculum depth and industry recognition. IMBS credentials are internationally accredited.

faqs.question: What support is available if I struggle with a module?
faqs.answer: Students have access to faculty office hours, a peer learning forum, and a dedicated academic support team to assist with any module they find challenging.

faqs.question: Is coding experience required before joining?
faqs.answer: No prior coding experience is required. The programme begins with computer fundamentals and introductory C programming before advancing to more complex topics.

faqs.question: Will I be able to build real apps by the end of BPCA?
faqs.answer: Yes. By Year 3, students complete a full web or desktop application project. The curriculum is progressively hands-on, ensuring you graduate with a practical portfolio.

faqs.question: Can I pursue MCA after completing BPCA?
faqs.answer: Yes. BPCA graduates are well-positioned to apply for the MCA programme at IMBS or other recognised institutions for advanced technical specialisation.

faqs.question: Are there live coding labs or only pre-recorded lectures?
faqs.answer: The BPCA combines pre-recorded theory lectures with scheduled live coding labs and interactive peer coding sessions to reinforce practical programming skills.

faqs.answer: Yes. The BPBA is an internationally accredited bachelor-level degree in business administration. IMBS graduates are employed across industries globally.
